title: Row vs Column Flow
description: Create a 3-column grid with 9 items. First use default flow (row), then switch to column flow. Observe how items 1-9 fill the grid differently.

solution.css: .grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 8px; max-width: 400px; grid-auto-flow: row; }

title: Dense Packing Gallery
description: Create a 4-column grid with 10 items where items 1, 4, and 7 span 2 columns each. Use dense packing to fill gaps. Then switch off dense to see the difference.
hint: Add `grid-auto-flow: dense;` to the gallery. Without dense, spanning items create gaps in the grid. With dense, items flow into those gaps.

title: Masonry-Style Portfolio with Dense Packing
description: Build a portfolio grid with 12 items of varying sizes (small, wide, tall, big). Use dense packing to create a masonry effect. Items 1, 4, 8, and 11 should be big (span 2×2). Items 3 and 7 should be wide (span 2 cols). Items 6 and 10 should be tall (span 2 rows). All other items are regular. The grid must fill all gaps without media queries.

starterCode.html:
1 BIG
2
3 WIDE
4 BIG
5
6 TALL
7 WIDE
8 BIG
9
10 TALL
11 BIG
12

hint: Use `grid-auto-flow: dense;`. For big: `grid-column: span 2; grid-row: span 2;`. For wide: `grid-column: span 2;`. For tall: `grid-row: span 2;`. The dense algorithm fills all gaps automatically.
